content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach makes two visible, well-timed reads that change the conversation. Turn 8 ('You're working pretty hard to disqualify both signals') names the intellectual-disqualification stance directly — catching the happiness filter / intellectual-question-with-emotional-body pattern operatively even without using the term. Turn 12 ('I notice you moved pretty quickly away from the question') is a textbook AoA read: observation language, named at the exact moment the client deflected, and the read precedes the re-asked question rather than being a blind follow-up. Both reads land and produce visible softening (longer pause, smile that doesn't reach, admission in turn 13). The coach is tracking stance, not just content. Missing is an explicit somatic read (the client offers jaw tightening, controlled voice, eyes dropping — coach doesn't name these), which keeps this from being a maximally rich 3, but the pattern-level reads of defendedness and deflection are specific, timely, and operative, which is what the rubric weights most.",
  "evidence_turns": [8, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Content-level orienting question; no read yet."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Redirects to body — implicit read that client is in their head, though not named as a read."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Follow-up question; doesn't name what the coach is noticing."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the stance directly: 'working pretty hard to disqualify both signals.' Catches the intellectual-disqualification pattern operatively."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good pivot question but not framed as a read."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Classic AoA read: 'I notice you moved pretty quickly away from the question' — observation language, named at the deflection moment, read precedes the re-ask."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Moves to history/content; misses the live somatic signals (jaw tightening, controlled voice) that client is offering."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "the-happiness-filter"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Coach reads stance well but underuses available somatic data (jaw, voice control, eyes dropping). Still meets the 3 bar via specific, timely, pattern-level reads that visibly shift the conversation."
}
